content.rendered:
“Sea of Heartbreak” first became a hit in 1961, when Don Gibson took it all the way to #2 on the Billboard Hot Country Singles chart.

It charted again eleven years later, when Kenny Price took it to #24.

…and again in 1989, when Ronnie McDowell took it to #39, marking three consecutive decades for with Top 40 country status for “Sea of Heartbreak.”

If only Johnny Cash had released it as a single when he recorded it for his 1996 Unchained album, “Sea of Heartbreak” might have charted for four consecutive decades.

He didn’t though, and the streak ended. But that didn’t stop Cash’s daughter Rosanne from taking a run at it herself in 2009.

Well, not exactly by herself. From Cash’s album, The List, here’s “Sea of Heartbreak” with Bruce Springsteen.

Rosanne and Bruce performed “Sea of Heartbreak” together just once, at Rosanne’s show in Durham, North Carolina in 2010. No recording circulates from that performance.

Sea of Heartbreak
Recorded: ?
Released: The List (2009)
First performed: April 15, 2010 (Durham, NC)
Last performed: April 15, 2010 (Durham, NC)

© January 12, 2018

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Ring of Fire
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Traditional, slow-grooving, or hard-rocking, they're all great: check out Bruce's three very different covers of Johnny Cash's "Ring of Fire" spanning five decades.

jetpack-related-posts.title: MatR: Bruce Springsteen and Robert Gordon: Heartbreak Hotel
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce covered it throughout the Darkness Tour, but his first performance of Elvis' breakthrough hit was with a musician the song inspired.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: I Walk the Line
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Twice only: Bruce paid tribute to The Man in Black after his passing by opening his shows with a lovely acoustic cover of "I Walk the Line."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Give My Love to Rose
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One of the rarer officially released Springsteen recordings, Bruce recorded "Give My Love to Rose" for a Johnny Cash tribute album and TV special.

jetpack-related-posts.title: October 6
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Bruce plays "Two Hearts" for the first time, and the E Street Band tackles "Kitty's Back" for the first time in 24 years. Plus: Bruce plays an Obama campaign rally and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Mountain of Love
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: An early, minor rock-and-roll hit proved a perfect fit for the young E Street Band.
